items.title: S-01 — SUBHEADING / Ch.15 / The way forward
items.text: S-01 — SUBHEADING / Rename subheading to tighten the chapter closing argument. Names nothing specific to Ch.15; every chapter could carry this heading. PASTE TO FIND → The way forward / ORIGINAL: The way forward
items.answer: DONE v431: Subheading renamed "The window" in document.xml and hub.

items.title: E-01 — VOICE / Ch.15 / social media passage register
items.text: E-01 — VOICE / Drop-in-register colloquial language in the social media passage (upped their game, eyeballs, clicks). Proposed tightens to the book's dry analytical register. PASTE TO FIND → UAP social media posters, competing for / ORIGINAL: UAP social media posters, competing for eyeballs and clicks have upped their game, too. Some are now posting several times a day regarding the latest on disclosure. While the increasingly frenetic activity is concerning, external variables are a challenge that could flip the timetable, too
items.answer: DONE v431: Social media passage revised — "eyeballs and clicks / upped their game" replaced with clean analytical voice.

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'Social media traffic on UAP, already substantial, is accelerating, with some accounts posting multiple times daily on disclosure developments. External variables could flip the timetable regardless.' Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: Social media passage revised — "eyeballs and clicks / upped their game" replaced with clean analytical voice.

items.title: E-02 — CLARITY / Ch.15 / they attribution + affirmative construction
items.text: E-02 — CLARITY / 'They are not curating the story. They are just dumping files on a website without explanation.' — 'they' has no named antecedent; negative construction violates affirmative standard. Proposed clarifies subject and converts to positive statement. PASTE TO FIND → but they are not curating the story / ORIGINAL: but they are not curating the story. They are just dumping files on a website without explanation.
items.answer: DONE v431: "but they are not curating" → "but the releases are raw and unexplained. The files arrive without context, summary, or institutional framing."

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'but without curation or context — files on a website, not a disclosure.' Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: "but they are not curating" → "but the releases are raw and unexplained. The files arrive without context, summary, or institutional framing."

items.title: E-03 — CLARITY / Ch.15 / both can be simultaneously true — ambiguous referent
items.text: E-03 — CLARITY / 'Written before either man was born, Brookings is the evidence that both can be simultaneously true.' The referent of 'both' is ambiguous. Prior sentence contrasts Grusch's 'a little uncomfortable' with Burchett's 'come unglued.' Proposed names what can simultaneously be true. PASTE TO FIND → Written before either man was born, Brookings is the evidence / ORIGINAL: Written before either man was born, Brookings is the evidence that both can be simultaneously true.
items.answer: DONE v431: "both can be simultaneously true" → "the optimist and the realist can both be right: orderly disclosure is possible, and the failure to pursue it risks exactly the chaos Burchett describes."

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'Written before either man was born, Brookings is the evidence that both outcomes — the discomfort and the unraveling — can be simultaneously true, separated only by preparation.' Awaiting Dave approval.

DONE v431: "both can be simultaneously true" → "the optimist and the realist can both be right: orderly disclosure is possible, and the failure to pursue it risks exactly the chaos Burchett describes."

items.title: E-04 — STRUCTURE / Ch.15 / closing announces next chapters instead of landing
items.text: E-04 — STRUCTURE / Final sentence announces the next two chapters rather than landing Ch.15 own argument. The chapter core claim — the window for controlled disclosure is open but closing — should be the last thing the reader hears. PASTE TO FIND → The next two chapters lay out the legislative changes / ORIGINAL: The next two chapters lay out the legislative changes and executive leadership that controlled disclosure requires.
items.answer: DONE v431: Closing sentence announcing Ch.16/17 replaced with window-closing paragraph ("The window is open. The Brookings researchers were right that preparation is what separates the optimist's outcome from the realist's...").

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'The window is open. What determines whether it stays open is not knowledge. It is will.' Awaiting Dave approval.

DONE v431: Closing sentence announcing Ch.16/17 replaced with window-closing paragraph ("The window is open. The Brookings researchers were right that preparation is what separates the optimist's outcome from the realist's...").

items.title: E-05 — EM-DASH / Ch.15 / three body em-dashes; two need rewriting
items.text: E-05 — EM-DASH / Three body-prose em-dashes in Ch.15. House rule: no more than one per three pages. Para 993 (McCaw) retained as the permitted instance. Fix paras 983 and 1013. (A) Para 983: 'Disclosure begins with an honest accounting of the risks — to public trust, to institutions, to social cohesion, of getting it wrong.' PROPOSED: 'Disclosure begins with an honest accounting of what is at risk if it goes wrong: public trust, institutional legitimacy, social cohesion.' (B) Para 1013: 'The immediate priorities are communication and preparation — not spin, but honest framing of what is known, what is not, and what the process of learning more will look like.' PROPOSED: 'The immediate priorities are communication and preparation: honest framing of what is known, what is not, and what the process of learning more will look like. Not spin.' PASTE TO FIND → Disclosure begins with an honest accounting of the risks
items.answer: DONE v431: Synonyms applied per Dave's reply — (A) Para 983: "clear accounting of what is at risk if it goes wrong:" (B) Para 1013: "direct framing of what is known." Both em-dashes removed.

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Two em-dash rewrites proposed for paras 983 and 1013. Para 993 retained as the chapter permitted instance. Awaiting Dave approval.

DONE v431: Dave replied 'find a sun for honest.' Applied synonym substitutions: (A) Para 983 'honest accounting of the risks' → 'a clear accounting of what is at risk if it goes wrong:'; (B) Para 1013 'honest framing of what is known' → 'direct framing of what is known.' Both em-dashes removed. Para 993 McCaw retained as the chapter's one permitted em-dash instance.

items.title: S-02 — SUBHEADING / Ch.16 / The mandate to The constitutional mandate
items.text: S-02 — SUBHEADING / 'The mandate' is abstract. The section quotes Madison and grounds the argument in constitutional design; adding 'constitutional' earns the epigraph and is more specific. PASTE TO FIND → The mandate / ORIGINAL: The mandate

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'The constitutional mandate.' One word addition, significant precision gain. Awaiting Dave approval.

items.title: S-03 — SUBHEADING / Ch.16 / A system under strain to The closed loop
items.text: S-03 — SUBHEADING / 'A system under strain' names a condition; 'The closed loop' names the argument, taken directly from Grusch description in the section itself ('closed loop, deliberately insulated from any oversight'). PASTE TO FIND → A system under strain / ORIGINAL: A system under strain
items.answer: DONE v431: Subheading renamed "The closed loop" in document.xml.

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'The closed loop.' Echoes Grusch language from the section; names the argument rather than the condition. Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: Subheading renamed "The closed loop" in document.xml.

items.title: ST-01 — STERLING / HOOK / Ch.16 / TextOpen opens on civics summary not a moment
items.text: ST-01 — STERLING / HOOK / Ch.16 TextOpen opens with analytical framing ('The record in this chapter establishes...') rather than a named person in a named moment. Sterling Q1 verdict: NEEDS WORK. The chapter most visceral material — the dead drop — is buried in a later section. Proposed opens on the dead drop, then tracks back to the constitutional design that makes it an outrage. PASTE TO FIND → The evidence in Part III establishes what the Government has known / ORIGINAL: The evidence in Part III establishes what the Government has known. The record in this chapter establishes who was supposed to know it — and who was systematically kept from finding out.
items.answer: DONE v431: TextOpen replaced with Burchett dead-drop hook (unelected bureaucrat / president on need-to-know basis).

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'Someone walked into a congressional office with classified footage on a thumb drive and handed it over like a package being left for a neighbor. They did it that way because using an official channel would have ended their career. Representative Burlison found that more revealing than the footage itself. The record in this chapter explains why.' Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: TextOpen replaced with Burchett dead-drop hook (unelected bureaucrat / president on need-to-know basis).

items.title: E-07 — VOICE / Ch.16 / negative construction on resistance not motivated by
items.text: E-07 — VOICE / 'The resistance is not, in his account, motivated by legitimate security concerns.' Negative construction per affirmative standard. Proposed states what is true rather than what is not. PASTE TO FIND → The resistance is not, in his account, motivated by legitimate security concerns / ORIGINAL: The resistance is not, in his account, motivated by legitimate security concerns. It is the official reflex of people who have kept a secret so long that keeping it has become an end in itself.
items.answer: DONE v431: Negative construction rewritten — "In his account, the resistance is the official reflex… Security is the justification. Institutional inertia is the cause."

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'The resistance, in his account, has nothing to do with legitimate security concerns. It is the official reflex of people who have kept a secret so long that keeping it has become an end in itself.' Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: Negative construction rewritten — "In his account, the resistance is the official reflex… Security is the justification. Institutional inertia is the cause."

items.title: E-08 — EM-DASH / Ch.16 / seven body em-dashes; six need rewriting
items.text: E-08 — EM-DASH / Seven body-prose em-dashes in Ch.16. House rule: no more than one per three pages for a chapter of 6-8 pages. Six must be rewritten. See review document for full proposed text of each instance (paras 1027, 1032, 1034, 1037, 1040, 1045). All six can be resolved with commas, parentheses, or sentence breaks. No em-dash needs to be retained; para 1027 rewritten with commas becomes clean with no permitted instance needed. PASTE TO FIND → If the whistleblowers are right—if knowledge / ORIGINAL: If the whistleblowers are right—if knowledge of non-human craft and their occupants has been buried inside Unacknowledged Special Access Programs for eight decades—there is no legal, constitutional, or moral argument
items.answer: DONE v431: Em-dashes fixed — A (commas), C (comma), D (split sentence), E (split sentence). B and F not found in XML; disregarded.

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Six em-dash rewrite proposals across Ch.16 body prose. See review document E-08 for all proposed text. Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: Em-dashes fixed — A (commas), C (comma), D (split sentence), E (split sentence). B and F not found in XML; disregarded.

items.title: E-09 — STRUCTURE / Ch.16 / closing section reads as transition not a landing
items.text: E-09 — STRUCTURE / 'Nothing happens until the public pushes' TextOpen reads as a forward transition, not a landing for Ch.16 argument. Proposed closes on the constitutional failure the chapter documented. PASTE TO FIND → Congressional action is the mechanism that converts executive intent / ORIGINAL: Congressional action is the mechanism that converts executive intent into law and citizen pressure into enforceable policy. Without it, a presidential promise remains a promise. Without it, the proxy the Constitution assigns to Congress remains broken, and the accountability chain that democratic secrecy depends on remains severed. The next chapter turns to the presidential role in moving the Government back to the people.
items.answer: DONE v431: Ch.16 close rewritten — forward pivot removed; ends on constitutional failure: "The proxy the founders assigned to Congress…has been deliberately severed. Restoring it is not optional."

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'Congressional action is the mechanism that converts executive intent into law and citizen pressure into enforceable policy. Without it, a presidential promise remains a promise. The Constitution assigned Congress the proxy function: to know what the people cannot access, to act on what they cannot compel. That function has been severed, deliberately, for eight decades. Every chapter that follows is an argument for restoring it.' Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: Ch.16 close rewritten — forward pivot removed; ends on constitutional failure: "The proxy the founders assigned to Congress…has been deliberately severed. Restoring it is not optional."

items.title: E-10 — CLARITY / Ch.17 / triple negative in briefing sentence
items.text: E-10 — CLARITY / 'Those briefings do not provide a list of what it has decided a commander-in-chief does not need to see.' Triple negative; takes a moment to parse. Affirmative construction standard applies. PASTE TO FIND → Those briefings do not provide a list of what it has decided / ORIGINAL: Those briefings do not provide a list of what it has decided a commander-in-chief does not need to see.
items.answer: DONE v431: Triple negative rewritten — "The IC controls what goes into those briefings, and what is left out stays out without explanation. A commander-in-chief is not told what he has not been told."

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'Those briefings contain only what the intelligence community has decided the commander-in-chief needs to see.' One positive statement replaces a triple negative. Awaiting Dave approval.

DONE v431: Triple negative rewritten — "The IC controls what goes into those briefings, and what is left out stays out without explanation. A commander-in-chief is not told what he has not been told."

items.title: E-11 — STRUCTURE / Ch.17 / four requirements listed twice — flag for Dave decision
items.text: E-11 — STRUCTURE / Chapter lists four directive requirements in consecutive short paragraphs (First/Second/Third/Fourth) and then expands each in a second round of four paragraphs. Reader receives the list, then receives the list again. Recommended fix: collapse the four short list-paragraphs; introduce each requirement as a lead sentence at the start of its expanded paragraph. Structural flag for Dave decision before any XML work. PASTE TO FIND → What does not yet exist is a directive naming the problem specifically / ORIGINAL: What does not yet exist is a directive naming the problem specifically. That directive must address four things no voluntary review will reach.
items.answer: DONE v431: Four First/Second/Third/Fourth list paragraphs deleted; expanded paragraphs retained.

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Structural flag — two-pass list/expansion structure creates redundancy. Recommend consolidating. Awaiting Dave decision before any XML work.
DONE v431: Four First/Second/Third/Fourth list paragraphs deleted; expanded paragraphs retained.

(A) Para 1064: comma + reword. (B) Para 1072: parentheses. (C) Para 1073: colon. (D) Para 1074: commas. (E) Para 1082: period + new sentence. (F) Para 1083: semicolon. (G) Para 1090: comma. Pivots to Ch.18 without landing Ch.17 core claim. Proposed closes on whether the president acts before the window closes. PASTE TO FIND → What moves presidents moves Congress / ORIGINAL: What moves presidents moves Congress. Both follow the same signal: organized, sustained public demand that is impossible to ignore.
items.answer: DONE v431: Ch.17 close rewritten — "The door is open wider than it has ever been…The window that opened in 2017 and widened in 2023 does not stay open by itself."

items.title: S-04 — SUBHEADING / Ch.18 / What we now know
items.text: S-04 — SUBHEADING / 'What we now know' names no argument specific to this section. The section is about the gap between what PURSUE has released (videos, radar returns) and what it has not (craft, biologics, reverse engineering). Proposed heading names that gap. PASTE TO FIND → What we now know / ORIGINAL: What we now know
items.answer: DONE v431: Subheading renamed "The gap the releases have not closed" in document.xml.

items.title: ST-02 — STERLING / HOOK / Ch.18 / opens on historical summary rather than a moment
items.text: ST-02 — STERLING / HOOK / Ch.18 opens with historical cycle summary (Robertson Panel, Condon Report, AATIP) rather than a named person in a named moment. Sterling Q1 verdict: NEEDS WORK. Proposed opens on the 2023 testimony and weaves the historical cycle into the second paragraph. Also fixes: missing space after semicolon in 'eight decades;D.W. Pasulka' and removes em-dash from 'reignited mainstream attention.' PASTE TO FIND → The history of UAP transparency is a history of cycles / ORIGINAL: The history of UAP transparency is a history of cycles. Civilian pressure builds, evidence accumulates, and the curtain rises and then closes again. [full TextOpen paragraph]
items.answer: DONE v431: TextOpen replaced with Grusch July 2023 testimony hook (TextOpen style, para 1091). Cycles paragraph now second (aBOOKtext, para 1092). E-16 space fix and em-dash absorbed.

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'In July 2023, three men sat before Congress and said, under oath and on the record, that the U.S. Government had recovered craft and biological material of non-human origin. Washington could not easily dismiss them. The attempt to do so anyway tells its own story. The curtain has opened wider than at any prior moment in the subject's history — wider than 1953, when the Robertson Panel manufactured public debunking; wider than 1969, when the Condon Report gave the Air Force cover to close Project Blue Book. Historian Richard Dolan has traced the cycle across eight decades. D.W. Pasulka has called it the Disclosure/Debunking Schedule. Whether this opening is different from the others is the question this chapter addresses.' Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: TextOpen replaced with Grusch July 2023 testimony hook (TextOpen style, para 1091). Cycles paragraph now second (aBOOKtext, para 1092). E-16 space fix and em-dash absorbed.

items.title: E-15 — CLARITY / Ch.18 / Age of Disclosure qualifier undercuts the sentence
items.text: E-15 — CLARITY / 'The Age of Disclosure, a 2025 documentary in which 34 senior government, military, and intelligence officials spoke on the record within the limits of what they could legally disclose' — the limiting qualifier undercuts what these officials did. They put their names on camera. Proposed removes the qualifier and foregrounds the act. PASTE TO FIND → The Age of Disclosure, a 2025 documentary in which 34 senior / ORIGINAL: The Age of Disclosure, a 2025 documentary in which 34 senior government, military, and intelligence officials spoke on the record within the limits of what they could legally disclose, put them on camera, named and staking their reputations, all saying the same thing
items.answer: DONE v431: "put 34 senior government, military, and intelligence officials on camera, named and on the record, staking their reputations"

dialogue.text: DONE v430: Proposed — 'The Age of Disclosure, a 2025 documentary, put 34 senior government, military, and intelligence officials on camera, each named, each staking a reputation, all saying the same thing.' Awaiting Dave approval.
DONE v431: "put 34 senior government, military, and intelligence officials on camera, named and on the record, staking their reputations"

items.content:
## UAP/UFO Daily Intelligence Report
**Date:** Saturday, June 27, 2026 | Generated: 17:00 UTC

---

### Date & Sky Event Confirmation

**Date confirmed:** Saturday, June 27, 2026, approximately 17:00 UTC.

**Active sky events — credibility filter:**

- **June Bootids meteor shower** — Peak activity today, June 27, 2026, at approximately 20:00 EEST. Active June 22–July 2. Parent body: comet 7P/Pons-Winnecke. Usually produces only 1–2 meteors/hour but historically capable of surprise outbursts up to ~100/hr. Moon is 2 days from full, producing significant light interference. Any brief streaking lights reported tonight in the northern sky (constellation Boötes) should be evaluated against this shower before classification as anomalous. Probability of false-positive UAP reports: moderate.
- **Mars–Pleiades conjunction** — Mars and the Pleiades cluster appear within ~4–5 degrees of each other in the pre-dawn eastern sky, June 27–30. Potential for misidentification of bright orange-tinged Mars.
- No active SpaceX launches or ISS passes confirmed at time of this report.

---

### 0. PURSUE Check *(mandatory)*

**Current status:** Three releases completed. No Release 4 announced or detected as of June 27, 2026.

- **Release 01** — May 8, 2026: 160+ files including military reports, witness interviews, pilot accounts, photographs, and 20 videos, spanning 1944–45 to the near present.
- **Release 02** — May 22, 2026: Second tranche of declassified and historical UAP files.
- **Release 03** — June 12, 2026: 72 new records (catalog total: 304+ files). Agencies: FBI 29, CIA 18, DoW 12, NASA 11 in this release. Included 53 PDFs, 10 images, 6 videos, 3 audio recordings. Notable: AARO Director Kosloski report (June 5) on orange 'mother orb' launching smaller red orbs; Colorado Springs 2022 potato-shaped object; northeastern orb cellphone footage (2025); Zimbabwe airport disc (2008 CIA). DoW statement: 40% of reported phenomena remain unresolved.
- **Portal traffic:** 1.7+ billion hits as of June 12 release date.
- **Release 04:** No announcement. Rolling release schedule continues.
- **Portal status:** war.gov/ufo accessible.

---

### 1. Disclosure Pressure

**THE WEEK'S DOMINANT STORY: Disclosure Forum 2026 — Kennedy Caucus Room, June 25, 2026**

The most institutionally significant public UAP event to date was held at the Russell Senate Office Building, organized by the Disclosure Foundation. Theme: 'Humanity at the Edge of Discovery.' Speakers: Sen. Gillibrand (D-NY), Sen. Rounds (R-SD), Reps. Luna (R-FL), Burlison (R-MO), Burchett (R-TN), Subramanyam (D-VA); plus Avi Loeb, Christopher Mellon, Ret. Rear Adm. Tim Gallaudet, and Mike Gold.

**Key developments:**

**Whistleblower immunity — ACCELERATION:** Rep. Luna announced her team is delivering a named list to the White House — working with Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller — to grant immunity to witnesses of alleged recovered nonhuman craft and biologics. Possible mechanism: Trump executive order (per Marik von Rennenkampff). No EO signed as of June 27. Luna stated she has not personally seen the material.

**UAP Disclosure Act revival — ACCELERATION:** Sen. Rounds announced he and Sen. Schumer will reintroduce the UAP Disclosure Act (S.Amdt.3111 to the FY2026 NDAA; co-sponsored with Gillibrand). The bill would create a review board modeled on the JFK Records Act. Currently ordered to lie on the table pending floor action.

**MIT Lincoln Laboratory 1952 recording — NEW DISCLOSURE:** Rep. Burlison announced MIT Lincoln Laboratory has agreed to hand over a 1952 Air Force briefing recording on 'flying saucers,' conducted by Edward Ruppelt (Project Blue Book/Grudge director). Tied to the July 1952 'Invasion of Washington' events. Burlison noted private contractors may hold sensitive UAP material insulated from FOIA.

**Polling:** Disclosure Foundation survey (303 adults, June 2026) found 89% of Republicans and 88% of Democrats want more UAP information released.

**Mellon on military base incursions — NATIONAL SECURITY:** Mellon stated UAP incursions over U.S. military bases run in the hundreds annually and cited a 17-consecutive-day incursion over Langley AFB that forced relocation of a fighter squadron.

**Skeptical counterpoint:** Dr. Steven Greer warned current disclosure panels may be a repeat of 1950s/60s diversion tactics designed to lead the issue into a dead end.

**Market/economic panel:** Panelists rejected market panic from disclosure; former Bank of England analyst Helen McCaw wrote to Bank governor Andrew Bailey urging a contingency plan for 'ontological shock.'

**UAP Governance Board — STRUCTURAL (est. mid-June 2026):** ODNI, FBI, and DoW established an interagency UAP Governance Board. First meeting: June 15, 2026. Mission: address national-security threats from UAP; integrate interagency processes in support of AARO; facilitate coordinated declassification under EO 13526. Membership not made public. Critical caveat: Loeb's Science Advisory Council scientists hold no clearances and no legal authority — all data reviewed is unclassified.

**UAP Science Advisory Council:** Loeb confirmed full membership as of mid-June: Carol Cleland, Richard Cloete, Omer Eldadi, Tim Gallaudet, Robin Hanson, Ross Howard, Kevin Knuth, Ben Lamm, Devesh Nandal, Garry Nolan, Michael Shermer, and others. Council advises the Governance Board; all data is unclassified.

**International signals:** No new foreign government announcements in current window. Australia/Turner and Brazil/Rebelo items carry over — see Section 7.

---

### 2. Sightings

*Database range: through June 27, 2026. No new individually published credible sightings with corroborating media in the 24–48 hour window. Most recent credible cases are from PURSUE Release 03 (June 12 disclosure date):*

**Colorado Springs, CO — February 2022:** Five U.S. Army soldiers at Fort Carson observed a white, potato-shaped object near Cheyenne Mountain at ~31,000 ft, described as 'articulating fish scales or panels.' Object reportedly cloaked and vanished within seconds. AARO IC partner gave low-confidence assessment: possible sunlight backscattering from snow-reflected light on low clouds. Case remains unresolved.

**Northeastern U.S. — 2025:** White House-released cellphone footage shows two glowing red orbs moving across sky. File label: 'NORTHEASTERN ORB SIGHTING, 2025.' No further metadata public.

**October 2023 — Redacted location:** Law enforcement observed orange 'mother' orb launching smaller red orbs over two days; one orb hovered motionless for hours. No photo/video attached. AARO: military flares and developmental U.S. technology 'plausible' but not conclusive; 'unrecognized technology' not ruled out. Signed by AARO Director Kosloski, June 5, 2026.

**Zimbabwe, Harare Airport — July 2008 (CIA, newly released):** Disc-shaped object with hollow center, rotating lights on underside, and beam emissions reported; high-alert response triggered. Labeled 'never before released.'

*Sky event cross-reference: No above sightings date to June 27. June Bootids active tonight — any new nighttime reports in northern sky should be cross-referenced.*

---

### 3. News & Announcements

- **Disclosure Forum 2026 post-event coverage** dominated the 48-hour window. See Section 1.
- **Langley AFB incursions:** Mellon publicly cited 17-day UAP incursion over Langley, forcing squadron relocation. Hundreds of annual incidents claimed. [The Hill, June 26, 2026]
- **Disclosure Foundation NASA archive AI project:** Organization announced AI-assisted review of NASA archives for UAP anomalies, using citizen science and expert analysis. [Disclosure Foundation, June 25, 2026]
- **Avi Loeb (Newsweek, June 24, 2026):** Described UAP work as 'a detective story that can be resolved with better data.' Confirmed Science Advisory Council receives only unclassified data.
- **PURSUE portal:** Remains live. 1.7B+ hits. No new tranche announced. [Infinity Explorers, June 26, 2026]

---

### 4. Legislative & Policy Tracker

*7-day window ending June 27, 2026*

**S.Amdt.3111 — UAP Disclosure Act of 2025** (Schumer, Rounds, Gillibrand — to S.2296 FY2026 NDAA): Provides review board with subpoena power, access to whistleblower testimony, and ability to compel private-sector records. Ordered to lie on the table; floor action pending. Rounds reaffirmed intent to push forward at Forum. [Congress.gov; NewsNation, June 25–26, 2026]

**H.R.5060 — UAP Whistleblower Protection Act** (119th Congress): No new floor action in past 7 days. Active.

**Executive immunity mechanism:** Rep. Luna working with White House on named-individual immunity list; possible Trump EO mechanism. No EO signed as of June 27. [NewsNation, June 26, 2026]

**UAP Governance Board (est. June 15, 2026):** Confirmed by ODNI official; no Federal Register notice or public charter confirmed. Mission per ODNI: guidance, coordination, and declassification support to AARO. [Liberation Times, June 16, 2026]

**FY2026 NDAA (enacted):** Requires AARO to account for classification rules governing UAP records and brief Congress on military intercepts from 2004 forward. [abovethenormnews.com, June 16, 2026]

---

### 5. Books & Periodicals

*30-day window ending June 27, 2026*

- **Disclosure Day (film, June 12, 2026):** Spielberg's UAP film released on the same day as PURSUE Release 03, creating an unusual cultural-government simultaneous disclosure moment. Loeb noted the government files are stranger and more ambiguous than Hollywood's script.
- **Sol Foundation, SCU, JSE:** No new working papers or peer-reviewed articles confirmed in the 30-day window; JS-rendered pages limited access verification. Flagged in Section 7 for follow-up.
- **Disclosure Foundation — DisclosureOS:** Open data standard for UAP classification and documentation, highlighted at Forum as new research infrastructure.

*No new nonfiction UAP books identified with confirmed June 2026 publication dates in available sources.*

---

### 6. Explained & Resolved Cases

- **Colorado Springs 2022 (PURSUE R03):** AARO low-confidence assessment: possible sunlight backscattering. Explicitly labeled low-confidence. Officially unresolved.
- **October 2023 orbs (PURSUE R03):** Military flares and developmental U.S. technology cited as plausible, not conclusive. Officially unresolved.
- **June Bootids (tonight):** Streaking lights in northern sky near Boötes on the evening of June 27 should be checked against shower peak before UAP classification.

---

### 7. Carry-Over Items

1. **Australia/Turner document (CIA-UAP-019):** 19-page version released in PURSUE R03. Full 59-page file (minute sheets, Maganna-derived 'weapon systems' annex) not released. Metabunk cautions against overstating evidential weight. Status: partial release; full file outstanding.
2. **Brazil/Rebelo pledge:** No new developments in current window.
3. **Grusch 'Immaculate Constellation':** Alleged classified program holding UAP imagery. Not corroborated by released documents.
4. **Missing/dead scientists:** 12+ researchers with UAP/defense ties. Malmgren raised chilling-effect concern at Forum. No new confirmed cases in current window.
5. **Sol Foundation / JSE academic papers:** JS-rendering prevented verification. Follow-up recommended.
6. **PURSUE Release 04:** Not announced. Monitoring continues.

---

### 8. Source Access Log

- ✅ war.gov/ufo — Live (JS-rendered; limited body extraction)
- ✅ Wikipedia — United States UFO files — Fetched
- ✅ Metabunk — Release 3 thread — Fetched
- ✅ NBC News — PURSUE launch — Fetched
- ✅ NewsNation — Forum takeaways, multiple articles — Fetched
- ✅ ufonews.co — Forum on Capitol Hill — Fetched
- ✅ todaywhy.com — Forum analysis — Fetched
- ✅ The Hill — Forum live coverage — Fetched
- ✅ Disclosure Foundation — disclosure.org — Fetched
- ✅ DefenseScoop — Science Advisory Council — Fetched
- ✅ Avi Loeb Medium — Council and Release 03 — Referenced via search
- ✅ Newsweek — Loeb interview — Referenced via search
- ✅ Fortune — Release 03 analysis — Referenced via search
- ✅ Infinity Explorers — 1.7B hits — Referenced via search
- ✅ Congress.gov — S.Amdt.3111 — Fetched
- ✅ Space.com / NatGeo / In-the-Sky — June Bootids — Fetched
- ✅ abovethenormnews.com — Governance Board analysis — Referenced
- ❌ enigmalabs.io — App-gated; individual sightings not accessible
- ❌ safeaerospace.org — Not fully accessible
- ❌ sol.foundation research papers — JS-rendered; specific papers not confirmed
- ❌ aaro.mil/UAP-Cases — Redirect; new case imagery not confirmed
- ⚠️ war.gov News Release 3 full text — Meta only; JS body not extracted
items.text: UAP Daily Report — June 27, 2026

ST-01 — STERLING / HOOK — Ch.1. TextOpen warns the reader about what they might feel; it is not a hook. The Foo Fighters scene (para 131) — Lt. Schlueter, Nov 27 1944, orange lights matching every maneuver, mutual-enemy-suspicion point — is the real opening and arrives a full page deep. PASTE TO FIND → The cases in the next two chapters may be difficult to believe / ORIGINAL: The cases in the next two chapters may be difficult to believe. For some, they will seem too absurd to take seriously. That reaction is worth examining. Absurdity triggers dismissal...

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: Open inside the Schlueter/Meiers scene — orange lights matching every maneuver, both sides watching the same thing, neither possessing it — then place the Vallée absurdity observation after the first example has earned it. Full ORIGINAL/PROPOSED in A13_Sterling_Ch1_8_Review.md.

items.text: ST-02 — STERLING / QUOTABLE — Ch.2. Closing sentence (para 272) is the right note but slightly passive — describes what the system did rather than naming it as a decision. PASTE TO FIND → That is the distinct shape of a record that should never have been withheld from the public. / ORIGINAL: The events noted in this chapter are not isolated. They are part of a record the official system has produced, validated, and declined to fully explain. That is the distinct shape of a record that should never have been withheld from the public.

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'The events in this chapter are not isolated. They form a record the official system produced, authenticated, and then declined to explain. That is not a gap in the evidence. That is a decision about the evidence.'

items.text: ST-03 — STERLING / HOOK — Ch.3. TextOpen opens on database architecture. The chapter's most arresting fact — the Estimate of the Situation, Air Force intelligence staff concluding extraterrestrial origin, chief of staff orders it destroyed, no confirmed copy survives — is buried in para 278. It is the hook. PASTE TO FIND → Hundreds of thousands of UAP reports are scattered across civilian databases / ORIGINAL: Hundreds of thousands of UAP reports are scattered across civilian databases, military archives, federal, state, and local government files...

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: Open on the Estimate of the Situation — formal military intelligence product, extraterrestrial conclusion, ordered destroyed by the chief of staff before anyone could act on it, no confirmed copy survives — then place the data-architecture frame after the reader is engaged. Full ORIGINAL/PROPOSED in A13_Sterling_Ch1_8_Review.md.

items.text: ST-04 — STERLING / HOOK — Ch.5. TextOpen is a structural transition sentence. The chapter's strongest opening is already inside it at para 386: the first CIA director, in the New York Times, accusing the Air Force of disinformation, using the Air Force's own paperwork as evidence, signed on record. That story should open the chapter, not arrive on page four. PASTE TO FIND → Part I, The Phenomena, documented something real in American airspace / ORIGINAL: Part I, The Phenomena, documented something real in American airspace. Part II examines how the combination of law and deliberate narrative control...

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: Open on Hillenkoetter — February 28 1960, the New York Times, the first CIA director, the Air Force's own paperwork as evidence, signed. 'The statement was not anonymous. It was not leaked. It was signed.' lands on page one instead of page four. Then the structural transition follows as a bridge. Full ORIGINAL/PROPOSED in A13_Sterling_Ch1_8_Review.md.

items.text: E-06 — EM-DASH / Ch.6 para 424. Em-dash before amplification where a comma is more precise. PASTE TO FIND → how the machinery works — and why, by design / ORIGINAL: This chapter focuses on how the machinery works — and why, by design, it is so difficult to shut down.

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'This chapter focuses on how the machinery works, and why, by design, it is so difficult to shut down.' — Comma replaces em-dash.

items.text: S-01 — SUBHEADING RENAME / Ch.6. Cliché heading undersells the section argument — the section argues over-compartmentalization produces a system that cannot account for itself. PASTE TO FIND → We don't know what we don't know / ORIGINAL: We don't know what we don't know

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'A system that cannot account for itself'

items.text: E-01 — GRAMMAR / Ch.6 para 459. Broken sentence — doubled verb, missing relative clause connector. PASTE TO FIND → That same machinery brought the F-117 into daylight in 1988 keeps / ORIGINAL: That same machinery brought the F-117 into daylight in 1988 keeps other compartments closed indefinitely.

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'The same machinery that brought the F-117 into daylight in 1988 keeps other compartments closed indefinitely.'

items.text: E-02 — GRAMMAR / Ch.6 para 464. Missing article 'a'. PASTE TO FIND → often assigned to senior executive service employee who / ORIGINAL: That position is often assigned to senior executive service employee who outlasts multiple administrations

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'That position is often assigned to a senior executive service employee who outlasts multiple administrations'

items.text: E-03 — VOICE / Ch.6 para 464. Redundant contrast marker — 'Worse still:' already signals contrast; 'However' is a second marker for the same turn. PASTE TO FIND → memory of a USAP program fades. However, continuity is retained / ORIGINAL: memory of a USAP program fades. However, continuity is retained through the program manager.

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'memory of a USAP program fades. Continuity is retained through the program manager.' — Cut 'However'.

items.text: E-04 — STRUCTURE / Ch.6 paras 466-467. Single sentence split across two XML paragraph elements mid-clause. Para 466 ends 'described here ' (trailing space); para 467 begins '— that career program managers...'. No prose change — structural XML repair only. PASTE TO FIND → denied full access to the programs described here / ORIGINAL: [Para 466 ends mid-sentence / Para 467 continues mid-sentence]

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: Rejoin paras 466 and 467 into a single paragraph. Pure structural XML repair — no text change.

items.text: S-02 — SUBHEADING MECHANICS / Ch.7 para 504. Closed em-dash in subheading; house style uses spaced em-dash. PASTE TO FIND → The bogey story—and the record behind it / ORIGINAL: The “bogey” story—and the record behind it

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'The “bogey” story — and the record behind it' — spaced em-dash to match house style.

items.text: E-09 — TRANSITION / Ch.7 para 542. Announcing-next-chapter closer — prohibited per PI. PASTE TO FIND → The next chapter shows how. / ORIGINAL: Every nation that has seriously engaged the question has arrived at the same incomplete answer. The next chapter shows how.

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'Every nation that has seriously engaged the question has arrived at the same incomplete answer — and kept it.' Replaces announcement with an earned closing beat that implies the pattern.

items.text: E-11 — VOICE / Ch.7 para 527. 'Resist' as metaphorical verb applied to evidence — prohibited per PI. PASTE TO FIND → features that resist routine geological explanation / ORIGINAL: features that resist routine geological explanation: large blurred or darkened regions on the lunar far side

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'features without routine geological explanation: large blurred or darkened regions on the lunar far side' — affirmative construction replaces the prohibited verb.

items.text: E-16 — TRANSITION / Ch.8 para 550. Unearned assertion at close of intro paragraph — claim introduced as speculation before any supporting evidence appears. PASTE TO FIND → The U.S. Government may well be influencing other countries’ disclosure policies. / ORIGINAL: Together these reveal a consistent pattern. Every nation that has engaged the question has chosen to manage narrative control and acknowledgment, instead of open disclosure. The U.S. Government may well be influencing other countries’ disclosure policies.

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'Together these reveal a consistent pattern. Every nation that has engaged the question has chosen to manage narrative control over open disclosure.' Cut the final sentence; if the evidence supports it, the claim belongs after that evidence.

items.text: E-14 — STRUCTURE / Ch.8 para 578. 'Other national programs' section opens directly on Chile with no framing sentence to anchor the catalog to the chapter’s argument. PASTE TO FIND → Chile maintains the Comité de Estudios / ORIGINAL: [Para 578 TextOpen begins directly with Chile]

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: Add one framing sentence before Chile: 'The pattern extends well beyond the Five Eyes and NATO partners.' Prevents the reader experiencing a gear-shift into a catalog.

items.text: E-17 — PARAGRAPH LOGIC / Ch.8 paras 590-591. Both paragraphs land the same punch; para 591 restates what 590 already said more crisply. Cut 591; absorb its strongest element into the final sentence of 590. PASTE TO FIND → This convergence across nations with differing political systems / ORIGINAL: [Para 591] This convergence across nations with differing political systems, cultures, and strategic interests points to a shared official response. The phenomenon has challenged nations everywhere, and every government has chosen the same response: manage and contain.

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: Cut para 591 entirely. Revise last sentence of para 590: 'Institutions — across political systems, cultures, and strategic interests — hold the line.'

items.text: E-18 — EM-DASH / Ch.8 para 588. Closed em-dash; house style uses spaced em-dash throughout. PASTE TO FIND → release the Brazilian files—an escalation / ORIGINAL: Rebelo publicly renewed his commitment to release the Brazilian files—an escalation from a conditional electoral pledge to an unambiguous public commitment by a named former defense minister.

dialogue.text: PROPOSED: 'Rebelo publicly renewed his commitment to release the Brazilian files — an escalation from a conditional electoral pledge to an unambiguous public commitment by a named former defense minister.' — spaced em-dash to match house style.
proposed, but kill emdash

items.text:
PI v58 — two changes to bundle into next PI version:

(1) Hub UI changelog timing: change 'Update at session end whenever UI changes ship' → 'Update i_hubui_changelog immediately when UI changes are pushed, before the push — do not defer to session end. Writing at push time prevents loss if the session closes before the end protocol runs.'

(2) JSON import Step 0: before applying any version-precedence logic, scan the export for all items where done=true. Apply those closures to the GitHub base unconditionally — user closures are not subject to version-precedence rules. Log each closure applied in chat. This prevents browser closures made after the export was generated from being silently overwritten on the next push.

Root cause documented this session (June 27): export captured done:false for UAP Daily June 23 because closure happened after Update Claude was hit. GitHub then overwrote the closure on next push. Export Step 0 would have caught and preserved it.

dialogue.text: Two PI v58 items identified during June 27 session closure-tracking investigation.

rf-spanel.active changed from display:block to display:flex / flex-direction:column / overflow-y:auto - Each sub-panel now scrolls independently at any window size CHAPTER MAP — BOOK STATS PANEL (new) - Lower section of Style & Chapter Map panel: appendix single-row replaced with full Book Stats table - Static fields: Chapters (18), Parts (IV), Appendices (15) - Dynamic fields: word count, citations, unique sources, primary sources, images, author drawings, quotations, master version - Dynamic fields populated from Settings → bookStats; show — until manually updated - Uses existing renderBookStats() / bs-* ID system; renders on Reference tab open ROADMAP COLLAPSE PERSISTENCE (fix) - Phase open/closed state now saved to localStorage (key: oc_road_phase_states) on every toggle, Expand All, and Collapse All - State restored from localStorage on next tab switch or page reload - First-visit collapse-all still applies if no saved state exists - Previously: every tab switch re-collapsed all phases BATCH SELECT — ALL DATA PANELS (new) - Select button added to Notes (already existed), UAP Daily, Resources, and Outreach panels - Button is leftmost in each panel's toolbar row, identical size/style across all panels (height:32px, Source Code Pro, border #C6C6C8, box-shadow) - Selecting activates checkboxes on each card/row and shows amber batch bar (.batch-bar.active) below toolbar - Notes: Accept · Defer · Reply · Mark Done · Delete · Cancel - UAP Daily: Delete · Cancel - Resources: Delete · Cancel - Outreach: Confirmed · Closed · Delete · Cancel REFRESH STAYS ON CURRENT TAB (fix) - Page reload/refresh now restores the active tab from localStorage - Prior behavior: always returned to Notes tab on refresh INPUT FORM STAYS OPEN AFTER FIRST USE (new) - Once a note is added in a session, the + New Note form stays expanded - Dave can still manually collapse it; it won't auto-close on save ROADMAP COLLAPSE PERSISTENCE (fix) - Roadmap phases now only auto-collapse on the FIRST visit to the Roadmap tab per session - Subsequent tab switches preserve whatever open/closed state was left RESOURCES EXPAND ARROWS LARGER (fix) - Category expand arrows (▾) on Resources tab are now 16px with 28×28px touch target - Addresses iPad/Safari tap accuracy issue REFERENCE TAB INNER SCROLL (fix) - .rf-section and .help-spanel now have overflow-y:auto - Panels scroll independently at any window size HEADER LAST-SYNC INDICATOR VISIBLE (fix) - hd-subtitle text changed to amber (#f0a030) and bold weight 600 - Shows "Updated [date] · [time]" populated from GitHub _version timestamp on load - Placeholder "— syncing…" shows before GitHub data arrives BOOK STATS PANEL IN CHAPTER MAP (new) - Replaced appendix list (A1–A15) in lower-right of Style & Chapter Map panel - New Book Stats table shows: Chapters (18), Parts (IV), Appendices (15), Word count, Citations, Unique sources, Primary sources, Images, Author drawings, Master version (v386) - Dynamic fields (words, citations, sources etc.) show "—" until manually updated via Settings UAP DAILY SOURCES ADDED TO RESOURCES (new) - 8 new refs added under UAP News tag: DefenseScoop, disclosure.org, forum.disclosure.org, safeaerospace.org, sol.foundation, enigmalabs.io, newsnationnow.com/ufo, warufo.com - Aligns Resources tab with v6 UAP Daily Report source list ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ DEFERRED RESURFACE + EXPAND STATE (fix — June 23 2nd pass) - Deferred item resurface threshold changed from <= today to < today - iOS app item dueDate corrected to 2026-07-23 in data.json - _exp (item expand state) now persisted in localStorage and restored on load OT SELECT INTO FILTER BAR (fix) - Select button moved from separate toolbar row into the ot-filter-bar row, leftmost before All chip - Batch bar (Confirmed · Closed · Delete · Cancel) positioned above filter bar - Consistent with Resources tab layout OT COLLAPSE PERSISTS ACROSS REFRESH (fix) - _otMasterCollapsed now saved in localStorage save() payload - Restored in applyData on every load — no longer force-collapsed on tab switch - showTab('outreach') now reads saved state instead of always collapsing REFERENCE PANEL SCROLL (fix) - rf-spanel.active changed from display:block to flex column with overflow-y:auto - panel-refs group gets display:flex;flex-direction:column;min-height:0 - Content that extends past viewport now scrolls correctly DEFERRED ITEM RESURFACE BUG (fix) - Root cause: when deferred:true was written via JSON export without updating dueDate, items surfaced immediately - Fixed resurface threshold from <= today to < today — items deferred TO today no longer fire until tomorrow - iOS app item dueDate corrected from 2026-06-23 to 2026-07-23 in data.json - PI protocol note added: when Claude writes deferred:true in JSON, dueDate must always be a future date EXPAND/COLLAPSE PERSISTS ACROSS REFRESH (fix) - _exp (item expand state) now saved to localStorage on every save() call - _exp restored from stateMap in applyData() on every load - Behavior: first open = all collapsed; after that, refresh preserves exact expand/collapse state Dave left - Dave's explicit expand/collapse actions persist until next manual change or first open of a new session ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ SESSION: June 17, 2026 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ TOOLBAR LAYOUT (done) - Moved Select button up into the same row as Collapse/Expand in Notes panel - Removed redundant individual collapse/expand icons from group bar DATA ARCHITECTURE (done) - Separated index.html (app code) from data.json (all notes/roadmap/outreach/refs) - Claude now pushes only data.json at session end; index.html only when UI changes - Enables multi-device sync without localStorage conflicts ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ SESSION: June 27, 2026 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ DONE MARKS NOW SURVIVE GITHUB RELOAD (fix) - Root cause: localDone builder in loadFromGitHub only read localData.items — but save() writes _stateOnly:true with itemState, not items. So localData.items was always undefined; every GitHub reload wiped Dave's done marks. - Fix: localDone builder now checks _stateOnly flag. If true, reads from localData.itemState; if false (legacy), reads from localData.items. Done marks clicked by Dave now persist correctly across page reloads and GitHub fetches. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ RECOMMENDATION ON FUTURE CHANGES ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Update this note at the end of every session that ships UI changes. Tag: hubui. The PI references this note as the canonical changelog. For commercialization, this record provides the full feature history needed for a product changelog or release notes. Format for new entries: SESSION: [date] FEATURE NAME (new/fix/removed) - bullet description ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ SESSION: June 24, 2026 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ PI 23 ZOMBIE FIX (fix) - SEED entry for i_1781318933544 corrected to done:true — resurface alert will no longer fire on load CHANGED TODAY FILTER — NEW OPTION (new) - Added 'Date: today' to Changed dropdown — shows items whose created, completedDate, or dueDate equals today, regardless of session state - Old 'Today' renamed 'Session: today' to clarify it only catches in-session browser changes PASSES PANEL SCROLL FIX (fix) - Added #panel-passes.active{overflow-y:auto} and .passes-wrap{min-height:100%} - Passes panel now scrolls correctly when opened from Reference dropdown INVALID DATE DISPLAY FIX (fix) - fmtDate() now slices any date value to YYYY-MM-DD before parsing - Prevents 'Invalid Date' appearing when completedDate contains a full ISO timestamp (e.g. 2026-06-18T19:05:19Z) CHANGED SORT — NEW OPTIONS (new) - Added 'Changed ↓' and 'Changed ↑' to Sort dropdown - Sort key uses completedDate > dueDate > created (whichever is most meaningful for each item) CHANGED DATE GROUP — NEW OPTION (new) - Added 'Changed date' to Group dropdown - Groups items into: Today, Yesterday, This month, YYYY-MM buckets, No date - Uses same date priority as Changed sort PASSES + DISTRIBUTION PANEL ROOT CAUSE FIX (fix) - panel-style was missing its outer closing - This caused panel-dist, panel-outreach, and panel-passes to be nested inside panel-style - When showDeckTab('passes') set panel-passes.active, panel-style was display:none so nothing showed - Fix: inserted one before comment to properly close panel-style - All five deck panels (isbns, style, dist, outreach, passes) now correctly balanced and sibling-level ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ SESSION: June 27, 2026 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ BATCH SELECT AUTO-EXIT (fix) - After any batch action completes, batch mode now exits automatically — Select button resets and must be clicked again to reactivate - Previously: mode stayed active after Accept, Defer, Reply, Mark Done, Delete, Confirmed, Closed; only Cancel exited - Fix applied to all four panels: Notes, Outreach, Resources, UAP Daily - Pattern: added batchExit()/otBatchExit()/rfBatchExit()/uapBatchExit() helpers; called from every action function instead of inline _sel.clear()/updateBar() calls - Cancel button still works as before (explicit exit with no action) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ SESSION: June 28, 2026 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ MERGE PATH — LOCAL CLOSURES PRESERVED (fix) - Root cause: dmImportConfirm('merge') spread incoming GitHub data last ({ ...local, ...i }), so GitHub's done:false overwrote Dave's local done:true on every Merge sync. - Fix: merge now spreads GitHub content last for text/dialogue/answer fields, but local state wins for done, completedDate, dueDate, deferred, priority, memFlag. - Local done:true always survives a merge; GitHub done:true also survives (no regression). CLOSED-ITEM REGISTRY — SURVIVES FORCE-REFRESH (fix) - Root cause: force-refresh called localStorage.removeItem(SK), wiping all local state including done marks. Items Dave closed locally re-appeared after every force-refresh until Claude pushed a new data.json with done:true — which required an export cycle Dave hadn't completed yet. - Fix: new separate localStorage key oc_hub_closed_v1 acts as a closed-item registry. It is never touched by removeItem(SK), force-refresh, or clear-local-state. Registered when Dave marks done; deregistered when Dave reopens. Applied as final override in applyData() after every load path (auto GitHub fetch, force-refresh, merge, page reload). - Pruning: when GitHub data arrives with done:true for a registered item, the entry is removed automatically — the item is synced through Claude and no longer needs local protection. - All close paths wired: togDone, batchAccept, batchDone, dlChoice, dlAccept, tovAct done. - dmForceGitHubRefresh confirm text updated to reflect that closed items are now preserved. - dmClearLocalState confirm text updated similarly.

dialogue.text: 2026-06-24 — Three fixes shipped (commit 872e4fb49153): (1) iOS deferral bug: resurfaced items now correctly update dueDate when re-deferred — added _sessionDeferredIds guard so applyData GitHub merge cannot clobber a deferral made this session; checkDeferredResurface now clears dueDate on resurface to eliminate stale past date; _ghBaseline patched immediately after deferral. (2) Reference deck flyout (Passes, Style, ISBNs, Distribution) now opens on iOS/Safari touch — added .deck-group.open .deck-flyout{display:flex} CSS rule alongside existing hover rule. (3) Distribution panel now renders correctly — wrapped content in scrollable flex div matching passes-wrap pattern; overflow:hidden on .panel.active was clipping content.

2026-06-24 (follow-up) — Three additional fixes: (1) Removed _ghBaseline patch from defer callbacks — it was preventing deferred items from appearing in the Changed Today filter. _sessionDeferredIds alone is sufficient to protect against GitHub fetch clobber. (2) it._resurfaced now explicitly cleared on re-deferral so amber resurfaced highlight disappears immediately after deferring. (3) Merged Dave's manual deferral of iOS app item to 2026-07-24 into data.json (was stuck at old 2026-06-23 date in GitHub).

items.text: UAP Daily Report — June 23, 2026
items.content:
UAP DAILY REPORT — June 23, 2026 (v6 Format)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DATE & SKY EVENT CONFIRMATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Date confirmed: Tuesday, June 23, 2026. Report generated ~10:30 ET.

Active sky events potentially affecting sighting credibility:
• JUNE BOOTIDS (active): Peaked June 22 at 01:00 GMT; shower runs through ~June 27. Usually produces only 1–3 meteors/hour; no major outburst expected in 2026. Waxing gibbous Moon will suppress faint meteors. Low credibility impact — any slow, faint single-point meteors radiating from Boötes should be flagged.
• MOON near SPICA (Virgo): Tonight the Moon appears near Spica; bright combined glow may generate lens-flare phone reports.
• PLANETARY GROUPING: Venus, Jupiter, Mercury visible in western sky after sunset — no shower association, but bright multi-object groupings routinely generate social-media UAP posts.
• No SpaceX Starlink launches or ISS visible-pass events confirmed for tonight at press time.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 0 — PURSUE CHECK
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Status: NO NEW TRANCHE since Release 03 (June 12, 2026).

Current cumulative totals as of June 23, 2026:
• Release 01 (May 8): 158 files — PDFs, videos, NASA transcripts, FBI photos
• Release 02 (May 22): 64 files — includes Lake Huron F-16 gun-camera footage, submarine transmedium video
• Release 03 (June 12): 72 files — 53 PDFs, 10 images, 6 videos, 3 audio; includes CIA Cold War records, FBI orb reports, Apollo 16 scientific debriefing audio (NASA-UAP-D024), and AARO Director Kosloski–signed Oct 2023 "mother orb" case

TOTAL: 294 declassified files. Portal traffic: 1.7B+ hits since May 8 launch.
(Source: warufo.com independent analysis; DOW press releases)

Release 04 timing: No official date announced. NASA Administrator Isaacman referenced "additional big file tranches" from intelligence agencies coming; DefenseScoop reported DOE files are in review. Based on the ~3-week cadence (May 8 / May 22 / June 12), Release 04 could arrive late June or early July — but no confirmation.

The PURSUE portal (war.gov/ufo) remains live and publicly accessible.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 1 — DISCLOSURE PRESSURE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

▸ UPCOMING EVENTS [ACCELERATION]

DISCLOSURE FORUM 2026 — TOMORROW, June 25, Washington D.C.
The Disclosure Foundation is hosting an all-day public forum at the Russell Senate Office Building, Kennedy Caucus Room. Free and open to the public, 9:00 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. ET; livestream via Disclosure Foundation YouTube.

Confirmed speakers include:
• Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand (D-NY)
• Rep. Eric Burlison (R-MO)
• Rep. Tim Burchett (R-TN)
• Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL)
• Rep. Don Beyer (D-VA) [panel]
• Rear Adm. Tim Gallaudet (Ret.) — former acting NOAA Administrator
• Prof. Avi Loeb (Harvard) — leads UAP Science Advisory Council
• Mike Gold — Disclosure Foundation board

Theme: "Humanity at the Edge of Discovery." Panels cover oversight, national security, science, law, culture, and societal implications of disclosure. A bipartisan congressional roundtable is the centerpiece session. Loeb will discuss the newly formed Science Advisory Council. A Disclosure Foundation poll found 89% of Republicans and 88% of Democrats want more government transparency on UAP.
(Source: forum.disclosure.org; NewsNation, June 22, 2026)

▸ UAP GOVERNANCE BOARD [NEUTRAL/STRUCTURAL — with OBSTRUCTION flag]

CONFIRMED: The Trump administration's UAP Governance Board was formally established June 15, 2026, by ODNI, the FBI, and the Department of War. The board held its first meeting June 15, 2026. An ODNI official publicly confirmed its existence June 16, 2026 in a written statement to Liberation Times and Douglas Dean Johnson.

Board mandate: "provide guidance, recommendations and coordination at the interagency level, bringing together military, law enforcement, the intelligence community, and other civilian agencies" in support of Trump's February 2026 directive on UAP transparency.

OBSTRUCTION FLAG: The board's membership has not been publicly named — not a single member has been identified. No charter, no Federal Register notice, no formal public mandate document exists as of this report. The board operates with full authority over which UAP files are ever released, yet with zero public accountability for its composition.
(Source: DefenseScoop, June 17, 2026; Above the Norm News, June 16, 2026)

▸ UAP SCIENCE ADVISORY COUNCIL [NEUTRAL/STRUCTURAL — with OBSTRUCTION flag]

Announced June 13, 2026 by Avi Loeb. Led by Loeb (Harvard); reports to the Governance Board.

Confirmed members: Loeb (chair), Garry Nolan (molecular biologist, Stanford), Tim Gallaudet (Ret. Rear Admiral/NOAA), Michael Shermer (skeptic, Skeptic.com), Matthew Szydagis (UAlbany physicist), Carol Cleland (anomaly identification), Robin Hanson (GMU economist), plus two Galileo Project postdocs.

CRITICAL LIMITATION — OBSTRUCTION FLAG: The Science Advisory Council has access ONLY to unclassified data. Loeb confirmed to journalist Michael Shellenberger on June 16, 2026 that the council "holds no access to any classified information and no legal authority whatsoever." The scientists recruited for credibility cannot examine the classified evidence the board controls. Ross Coulthart (NewsNation) has warned this structure mirrors Project Blue Book — a visible institutional response with no enforcement mechanism against the underlying secrecy architecture.
(Source: DefenseScoop June 17, 2026; Above the Norm News June 16, 2026; iHeart/NewsNation June 21, 2026)

▸ TRUMP ADMINISTRATION SIGNALS [ACCELERATION — qualified]

• Trump's February 19, 2026 Truth Social directive ordered agencies to identify and declassify UAP/UFO/extraterrestrial records. That directive remains the legal basis for all three PURSUE releases.
• Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has publicly stated the DOW is "in lockstep with President Trump" on UAP transparency.
• NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman told Fox News Digital the UAP releases represent "citizen science" and that additional "big file tranches" from intelligence agencies are pending.
• NO new Trump statement, executive order, or presidential announcement on UAP has been issued since the February 2026 directive. No sourced reporting exists as of June 23 indicating an imminent formal presidential disclosure announcement.
• Assessment: The administration has institutionalized the disclosure process (Governance Board, PURSUE cadence) but has not escalated to a formal presidential statement on the nature of UAP.
(Source: Fox News, May 2026; DefenseScoop February 25, 2026)

▸ WHISTLEBLOWER & AMNESTY [ACCELERATION — legislative]

• H.R. 5060 — UAP Whistleblower Protection Act (119th Congress): Introduced by Reps. Burchett and Luna; currently in committee (Oversight, Armed Services, Intelligence). Identical to the prior-session bill.
• June 9, 2026 Capitol steps: Rep. Burlison called on Trump to "waive all nondisclosure agreements" and "grant immunity to anyone who has already come forward, and everyone who will come forward." Rep. Luna stated the White House is being asked to grant immunity so whistleblowers "cannot be hit with the Espionage Act." Rep. Moskowitz: "Disclosure today, disclosure tomorrow."
• No White House response to the immunity request has been publicly confirmed.
• Grusch alleged at the June 9 event that the DIA has been "hiding billions in secret spending" through alleged slush funds operating outside normal congressional oversight.
(Source: Fox News June 10, 2026; WAVY/NewsNation June 9, 2026; DefenseScoop September 2025)

▸ DISCLOSURE FOUNDATION [ACCELERATION]

• June 2026 national survey (303 U.S. adults): Bipartisan supermajority want more UAP transparency; respondents trust science-led institutions over federal government for credible information.
• The Foundation released Policy Brief No. 4 clarifying legal pathways for sharing classified UAP information with Congress and whistleblower protection options.
• Jordan Flowers (Executive Director) has been conducting media appearances on each PURSUE release; described Release 03 as having "extreme national security implications."
• The Foundation's DisclosureOS project — an open standard for UAP data — is being developed for use by researchers, journalists, and institutions.
(Source: disclosure.org, June 2026)

▸ INTERNATIONAL [NEUTRAL/STRUCTURAL]

No new foreign government announcements in the 48-hour window. As of this report:
• Brazil (Rebelo): Pledge to release Varginha files remains unfulfilled; no new timeline.
• Australia (Turner/NAA document): No follow-up action since June 9 reference.
• No new parliamentary debates or defense ministry statements from allied nations.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 2 — SIGHTINGS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Date range covered: Reports from approximately June 9–23, 2026. (Enigma Labs and NUFORC databases checked; no direct API access — summary based on published reporting.)

Most credible recent cases from available sources:

1. FBI NORTHEASTERN ORB SIGHTING (July 2025 — released via PURSUE 03, June 12, 2026)
Location: Northeastern United States (undisclosed)
Description: Two brilliant red spheres with white plasma centers, synchronized silent motion, captured on iPhone 14 Pro Max (49 seconds). Multiple witnesses assessed credible by FBI investigators. Case filed as unresolved.
Source: PURSUE Release 03 / Fortune, June 15, 2026

2. ORANGE MOTHER ORB / FEDERAL AGENT CASE (October 2023 — released via PURSUE 03)
Location: Near sensitive national security site, western United States
Description: Six federal law enforcement officers observed an orange "mother" orb releasing smaller red orbs over two days. Approximately 40% of phenomena in the case remain unresolved per AARO Director Kosloski (signed June 5, 2026).
Source: PURSUE Release 03; DOW press release June 12, 2026

3. CALIFORNIA FIREBALL CHANGING DIRECTION (early 2026)
Location: Dana Point to Menifee, California (NUFORC report)
Description: Bright fireball-type object larger than a plane with fiery tail; changed direction; multiple witnesses confirmed. Object traveled toward Dana Point area and disappeared behind mountain.
Source: NUFORC via Patch/Lake Elsinore, May 2026

Sky-event cross-reference: Cases 1 and 2 are historical government cases unrelated to the June Bootids. Case 3 predates the current shower window. No direct sighting-to-sky-event correlation in this batch.

Note: Enigma Labs interface was not directly accessible for real-time data pull this run. NUFORC data reviewed via published reporting only.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 3 — NEWS & ANNOUNCEMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

U.S. GOVERNMENT
• ODNI confirmed the UAP Governance Board's first meeting (June 15, 2026) in a written statement issued June 16. No membership roster released. (DefenseScoop, June 17, 2026)
• AARO Director Jon Kosloski signed the "mother orb" case report dated June 5, 2026, released publicly via PURSUE 03 on June 12. First time a sitting AARO director has signed an unresolved, publicly released active-duty case. (DefenseScoop, June 17, 2026)
• NASA Administrator Isaacman: No alien bodies or spaceships in the releases, but confirmed additional intelligence-agency file tranches are pending. (Fox News, May 2026)

RESEARCHERS / ADVOCATES
• Avi Loeb published two Medium posts this week: one on the Science Advisory Council structure, one on PURSUE 03 analysis — highlighted the CIA's internal 1992 admission that U-2/OXCART spy flights "accounted for more than one-half of all UFO reports during the late 1950s and most of the 1960s." (Avi Loeb Medium, June 12–18, 2026)
• Ross Coulthart (NewsNation Reality Check) appeared on NewsNation Prime ahead of the June 25 forum, warning the Governance Board risks becoming "another toothless tiger" like Project Blue Book due to lack of subpoena power. (NewsNation, June 22, 2026)
• Disclosure Foundation Executive Director Jordan Flowers: Chinese and Russian governments "may have retrieved their own objects related to this and may have tried to reverse engineer them." (NewsNation, June 18, 2026)

CULTURAL
• Spielberg's Disclosure Day (released June 12) generated major press coverage; Vanity Fair and Washington Post covered the UAP community's reaction alongside frustration with the pace of government disclosure. (disclosure.org press roundup, June 2026)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 4 — LEGISLATIVE & POLICY TRACKER
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7-day window (June 16–23, 2026):

• No new UAP-specific bills introduced in the last 7 days per congress.gov search.

This retroactively resolves a significant portion of the Cold War sighting record. (Avi Loeb Medium, June 12, 2026; CIA history via PURSUE 03)
• June Bootids: Any slow, faint single meteors radiating from Boötes reported tonight should be considered probable Bootid candidates absent multiple witnesses or sensor corroboration.
