road.label: Write/Edit
road.note: Manual writing and mechanical cleanup passes

road.label: Mechanical Pass
road.note: Word for Word, Thematic Echos, AI removal

road.label: Tier A — Prose Pass
road.note: Full prose pass across all chapters. Trigger: "Prose pass [scope]". Covers: A1 Word-by-word, A2 Spelling/grammar, A3 AI-speak, A4 Paragraph logic, A5 Paragraph structure, A6 Flow check, A7 Thematic echo, A8 Sterling commercial read. Run before any Tier B work on the same scope.

road.label: Full Tier A Pass
road.note: Run all A-passes sequentially.

road.label: Tier B — Mechanical Pass
road.note: Mechanical verification passes. Run after Tier A is confirmed clean. Each sub-pass is discrete and logs on completion.

road.label: B1 — Formatting Pass
road.note: Style ID compliance, image placement rule, caption construction, section breaks, pull quote/Attribution pairs, TextOpen after subheadings, chapter structure order, two-space title normalization.

road.label: B2 — Cross-Reference Pass
road.note: Every "see Chapter N" and "Appendix AX" reference verified to resolve to real content with matching material. Back matter included. Logs to CrossRef_Verification_Log.md.

road.label: B3 — Endnote & Citation Pass
road.note: Full endnote and citation pass. Absorbs former B2 Citation Pass and former B4 Footnote Pass. Strips vestigial ref_/note_ bookmarks and return arrows; reads body text clean; builds claim inventory; matches to endnote pool; researches missing citations in-pass; resequences; Chicago style audit; URL wrapping. See PI v36 for full 7-phase protocol.

road.label: B4 — Word Endnote Conversion Pass
road.note: Converts all afootnote plain paragraphs to genuine Word endnotes (w:endnote elements) that Vellum can recognize and auto-link. Run after all prose locked, before index work (B7). Verify afootnote style has no numPr before conversion.

road.label: B5 — Appendix Pass
road.note: A2 sightings scan, A12 figures/diagrams, A15 Quotations location codes, A15 Bibliography new entries, Glossary new terms, Index new subjects. Run after B2 confirmed for relevant chapters.

road.label: B6 — URL Pass
road.note: Every hyperlink verified to resolve. Invisible-hyperlink rendering confirmed (color=auto, u=none, no Hyperlink style).

road.label: B7 — Index Pass
road.note: Run last — after all content is locked. Locator codes, bookmark naming, subentry formatting, print vs. Kindle locator styles, exclusion rules (A15, A12). Editing body content after B7 potentially invalidates locators.

road.label: Appendix sequence change A2, A12
road.note: Appendix sequence rationalization: consolidate reference/lookup appendices (A2, A12) into final grouping per filed remap doc requires a full cross-reference cascade pass.

road.label: Tier C — Production Pass
road.note: Final production passes. Run only after Tier A and Tier B are confirmed clean for all content. C0 precedes C1 and C2.

road.label: C0 — Final Darlings Review
road.note: One full read for anything missed. Minor prose only — no structural changes. Sterling final commercial assessment.

road.label: C1 — Production Pass Ebook
road.note: Heading hierarchy, TOC integrity, front/back matter, metadata, cross-reference integrity, image resolution (72–150 DPI), table simplification, line break removal, hyperlink audit. Status: Done v230.

road.label: C2 — Production Pass Print
road.note: Image resolution (300 DPI), color/grayscale audit, margin/gutter (6″×9″, 1″ margins), page breaks on recto, widows/orphans, index locator style (sz=16 print override), print index completeness.

road.label: Flow — continuity and rhythm read
road.note: Flag repetition, weak transitions, register drift.

road.label: Reese — sourcing integrity pass
road.note: Any unsourced factual claims surfaced by fresh read?

road.label: Sterling — commercial coherence read
road.note: Argument arc, chapter transitions, author positioning.

road.label: Prose locked — no further content changes after this point
Read for interest, alignment with marketing approach
Family Vacation
road.note: Scheduled break June 30 – July 8. No project work. Pitch-ready target is before this date.

road.label: Family vacation — no project work
road.note: June 30 through July 8 inclusive.

road.label: Export ebook via Vellum
road.note: Generate EPUB. Verify footnote navigation, image rendering, TOC.

road.label: Export print PDF via Vellum
road.note: Verify trim, bleed, margins, running heads, image resolution.

road.label: Order and review proof copy
road.note: Order proof from KDP or IngramSpark before approving for distribution.

road.label: Layout
road.note: Style, link checks

road.label: QA
road.note: Check all pages, flow, links, formatting

road.label: Export for Epub
road.note: Test read in Kindle

road.label: Agent/Publisher Decision and Pitch
road.note: 60-day pitch window to PublicAffairs, Dutton, Twelve, and one other. Lead with June 9 and Turner document. Differentiation: analytical/structural book vs. Elizondo memoir. Hard deadline: if nothing compelling by response window close, proceed with self-publishing. Book must be pitch-ready before family vacation June 30.

road.label: Pitch decision — go/no-go and target list
road.note: Decide whether to pitch and finalize list of 3–4 publishers (PublicAffairs, Dutton, Twelve + one). Make this call before prose is locked.

road.label: Build pitch package
road.note: Query letter, 2-page synopsis, sample chapters (suggest Ch. 1 + Ch. 9 or Ch. 16). Lead with June 9 Capitol steps and Turner document as differentiation hook.

road.label: Send to publishers — pitch window opens
road.note: Submit to all targets simultaneously. Log submission dates.

road.label: Publisher response window
road.note: Allow 60 days from submission. Follow up at 30 days if no acknowledgment.

road.label: Agent Pitch
road.note: Determine if any interested agents and then decide whether to self-publish

road.label: Response window closes — proceed to self-publish if no compelling offer
Update over-classified.com book description
road.note: Reflect June 9 Capitol press conference, Turner/NAA document, Aldo Rebelo/Varginha confirmation, PURSUE release milestone, and current disclosure momentum. Must be live before publisher pitch submissions go out.

road.label: Develop channel plan
road.note: Social media, messaging, methods

road.label: Develop blurb candidates list
road.note: Contact information, tailored approaches

road.label: Build blurb pitch sheet
road.note: Title · subtitle · 150-word synopsis · 100-word bio · pub target window.

road.label: Congressional — Burlison · Burchett · Luna
road.note: First wave — allow ~10 weeks.

road.label: Elizondo · Mellon outreach
road.note: Second wave — ~6–8 weeks.

road.label: Coulthart · Knuth · Loeb outreach
road.note: Third wave — validation tier.

road.label: Literary Agent Query
road.note: Query literary agents before or alongside publisher pitch. All three target publishers (PublicAffairs, Dutton/Caliber, Twelve) require agent submission. Top targets: Keith Urbahn (Javelin), Eric Lupfer (Calligraph), Matt Latimer (Javelin). 60-day query window. If agent secured before vacation, they handle publisher submissions. If no agent by Aug 29, proceed direct self-publish.

road.label: Agent decision — go/no-go on querying
road.note: Decide whether to pursue agents simultaneously with or before publisher pitch. Agents can submit to publishers more effectively and negotiate better terms. Time cost: 2–4 weeks to query; 4–8 weeks typical response.

road.label: Build query letter + 25-page sample
road.note: Query letter: 1 page. Hook (June 9 + Turner/NAA document), premise (analytical/structural vs. memoir wave), author credentials, chapter outline, word count. Sample: Ch.1 + Ch.5 opener. Calligraph: paste 25 pages in email body, no attachments, submissions@calligraphlit.com, name agent in subject. Javelin: check javelindc.com for current query instructions.

road.label: Query Urbahn + Lupfer + Latimer simultaneously
road.note: All three can be queried at once — no exclusivity requirement at query stage. Urbahn/Latimer (Javelin): national security insider nonfiction lane. Lupfer (Calligraph): explicitly lists national security and defense as focus; fastest known response time (1 day on record). Lead pitch: differentiation from Elizondo memoir wave + June 9 Capitol event + Turner/NAA document.

road.label: Agent response window
road.note: Allow 6–8 weeks from submission. Follow up at 4 weeks if no acknowledgment. If agent offers representation, evaluate deal terms before committing.

road.label: Agent decision — sign or proceed self-publish
Establish accounts
road.note: Set up relationships and learn process

road.label: KDP setup — Kindle and POD
road.note: Upload EPUB and print PDF. Do not enroll KDP Select.

road.label: Draft2Digital — wide ebook distribution
road.note: Enable all retail channels including OverDrive/Libby.

road.label: IngramSpark — print to bookstores
road.note: Upload print PDF and cover PDF. Set 55% trade discount.

road.label: Bowker metadata · website updates · press kit
road.note: Update subtitle, LCCN, categories. Favicons, hero images, press kit.

road.label: Update PURSUE references — final check before upload for sale
road.note: Final refresh of all PURSUE references in manuscript to reflect most current file drop counts and media buzz before uploading to distribution platforms.

road.label: Learn real requirements from Hilton Head Island
Update EIN number Record with new Vigilia Name
Finalize business licensing filings in time for book sales
Move Hub to Vercel
road.note: Fix conflict between over-classified.com (Vercel) and hub (GitHub Pages) by moving hub to Vercel. Four steps — takes about 30 minutes total. No coding required.

road.label: Step 1 — Create Vercel account & import oc-hub repo
road.note: Go to vercel.com → Sign Up → Continue with GitHub → Import the brownd11-a11y/oc-hub repository. No settings to change — Vercel detects it is a static site automatically. Click Deploy.

road.label: Step 2 — Assign hub.over-classified.com in Vercel
road.note: In your new Vercel project → Settings → Domains → Add: hub.over-classified.com. Vercel will show you a CNAME value to copy (looks like: cname.vercel-dns.com). Leave this tab open.

road.label: Step 3 — Add CNAME at your DNS registrar
road.note: Log in to wherever you bought over-classified.com → DNS settings → Add record: Type=CNAME, Name=hub, Value=paste what Vercel gave you in Step 2. Save. Takes up to 1 hour to propagate.

road.label: Step 4 — Remove custom domain from GitHub Pages
road.note: Go to github.com → brownd11-a11y/oc-hub → Settings → Pages → Custom domain → clear the field and Save. Hub push workflow unchanged — Claude still pushes index.html; Vercel redeploys automatically.

road.label: W3.1 — Mechanical pass rescan
road.note: Re-run against chapters edited since v229.

road.label: W3.8 — Word-by-word rescan
road.note: Focus on recently touched sections.

road.label: W3.9b — Production pass print
road.note: Vellum print workflow: 6×9 trim · image resolution 300 DPI · widows/orphans · recto breaks · index sz=16 · IngramSpark + KDP submission · physical proof.

road.label: Update PURSUE references — file drop counts + media buzz
road.note: Refresh all PURSUE references in manuscript body to reflect latest counts of file drops and media coverage at time of edit.

road.label: Word for Word on entire book
Thematic echo scan
road.note: Flag arguments or phrases appearing in substantially similar form across chapters.

road.label: No material repetition — Dave reviews and approves any cuts
Export print file for test run
Book Mechanically Ready for Final Manual Edits
Manual Edits
road.note: After all mechanical work is done, final read through for clarity, color and logic

road.label: Changes to accommodate current events
road.note: Decision on how and where to handle "Pressure Cooker" chapter or subtitle, updates based on current events

road.label: Content_Changes_Log retroactive sweep (prerequisite for B8)
road.note: Read all prior session notes, extract all new citations/endnotes/glossary/index additions since v229, build complete log entries by chapter. One-time task — half session. Required before B8 can run.

road.label: B8 — Back Matter Changes Pass
road.note: Consolidation pass — applies all entries in Content_Changes_Log.md: endnote markers, bibliography entries, glossary updates, index candidates, cross-reference verification. Requires: (1) retroactive sweep complete; (2) all prose locked; (3) Dave explicit go. Run after C0 Final Darlings, before C1 ebook export.

outreach.name: Eric Burlison
outreach.role: U.S. Rep., Missouri 7th — UAP Caucus / Task Force

notes.text: 1108 Longworth HOB, Washington DC 20515. (202) 225-6536. Most active House UAP Caucus member on legislation and dark-money financing of programs. Co-sponsored UAP Transparency Act 2024. Introduced bill to abolish AARO Apr 2026. June 9 Capitol press conference speaker. Approach: lead with the constitutional accountability framing — his language. Mention the F-117/NDA closing argument in Ch.6 directly.

outreach.name: Tim Burchett
outreach.role: U.S. Rep., Tennessee 2nd — UAP oversight, DOGE subcommittee chair

notes.text: 1110 Longworth HOB, Washington DC 20515. Web form requires district address verification — non-constituents should write direct mail or use press contact. Long-standing UAP advocate, first major House hearing organizer. June 9 Capitol press conference speaker. Approach: plain-spoken directness matches his style — lead with "the Government has known and won't say" framing, not academic language.

outreach.name: Anna Paulina Luna
outreach.role: U.S. Rep., Florida 13th — Chair, Task Force on Declassification

notes.text: 226 Cannon HOB, Washington DC 20515. Press contact: David Leatherwood — David.Leatherwood@mail.house.gov. First Mexican-American woman from Florida, Air Force veteran, chairs Task Force on Declassification of Federal Secrets. June 9 immunity announcement speaker — quoted in book. Approach: lead with the immunity/whistleblower angle from Ch.18 and Conclusion. Her own words are in A15.

outreach.name: Lue Elizondo
outreach.role: Fmr. AATIP director / NYT bestselling author (Imminent)

notes.text: Contact via official site form. Also active on X @LueElizondo. His book Imminent (HarperCollins, Aug 2024) is the dominant insider memoir in this space — NYT bestseller. This book is the analytical/structural complement to his memoir, not competition. Approach: differentiation is key — position as the book that explains the system Elizondo navigated, not another insider account. Mention Ch.9 Manufacturing Doubt and the Grusch smear parallel directly. FLAG: retrieve Elizondo past-chat context before drafting outreach.

outreach.name: Christopher Mellon
outreach.role: Fmr. Dep. Asst. Sec. Def. for Intelligence / UAP advocate

notes.text: Website: christophermellon.net. Active on X. Keynote speaker at 2026 SCU Conference (Toronto, July 24-26). Called AARO Volume I "the most error-ridden and unsatisfactory government report I can recall reading." Strongly critical of AARO — directly aligned with book's Ch.6 and Ch.10 arguments. Approach: lead with the AARO failure analysis and the classification architecture argument. He will recognize the structural argument immediately.

outreach.name: Ross Coulthart
outreach.role: Investigative journalist — NewsNation Reality Check / author

notes.text: Website: rosscoulthart.com. X: @rosscoulthart. NewsNation special correspondent hosting Reality Check. Broke the Grusch story in June 2023 exclusive. Five-time Walkley Award winner. Likely receptive — this book supports the accountability argument his journalism makes. Approach: emphasize the primary-source architecture and the Turner/NAA Australian document, which connects to his investigative interests.

outreach.name: Kevin Knuth
outreach.role: Prof. of Physics, Univ. at Albany — UAPx VP Science & Technology

notes.text: Email: kknuth@albany.edu. Phone: (518) 442-4501. Knuth Information Physics Lab, Physics 228, University at Albany, 1400 Washington Ave, Albany NY 12222. Co-author of landmark Entropy paper on UAP flight characteristics (cited in Ch.13). VP Science & Technology at UAPx — co-founded with USS Princeton veterans. Approach: lead with the Entropy paper citation and how the book frames his work within the broader institutional response to scientific UAP research.

outreach.name: Avi Loeb
outreach.role: Harvard Prof. of Science — Galileo Project founder

notes.text: Email: aloeb@cfa.harvard.edu. Staff assistant Uma Mirani: (617) 496-5870. Center for Astrophysics, 60 Garden Street MS-51, Cambridge MA 02138. Frank B. Baird Jr. Professor of Science. NYT/WSJ bestselling author (Extraterrestrial, Interstellar). Top 3 astronomer worldwide by publication impact (ScholarGPS 2024). Top-5 TED talk 2024. Approach: he has been explicit that science must take UAP seriously — frame this book as the institutional accountability argument that explains why the scientific community was shut out for 80 years.

outreach.name: PublicAffairs Books
outreach.role: Publisher — Hachette / serious nonfiction, current affairs, public policy

notes.text: Publisher: Clive Priddle. Part of Hachette Book Group. Publicity: PEmail@hbgusa.com. Backlist: Surveillance Capitalism (Zuboff), Rise of the Warrior Cop (Balko), The Jakarta Method. AGENT REQUIRED — no unsolicited submissions. Perfect fit: serious nonfiction about government accountability and institutional failure. Differentiation pitch: analytical/structural book vs. Elizondo memoir. Lead with June 9 and Turner/NAA document. Hachette HQ: 1290 Avenue of the Americas, New York NY 10104.

outreach.name: Dutton Caliber
outreach.role: Publisher — Penguin Random House / military, national security, intelligence

notes.text: Publisher: John Parsley (VP/Editorial Director). Caliber is Dutton's military/national security imprint — ideal fit. Boutique list ~40 books/year. AGENT REQUIRED. Backlist: numerous NYT bestsellers in national security/military nonfiction. Approach: pitch as a serious national security accountability book, not a UAP book. The Caliber imprint specifically targets this audience. Parsley edits narrative nonfiction; Pilar Garcia-Brown handles "deeply researched stories that engage with contemporary issues."

outreach.name: Twelve Books
outreach.role: Publisher — Hachette / singular books, unique perspectives

notes.text: Part of Grand Central Publishing / Hachette. Publishes only 12 titles per year — extremely selective. AGENT REQUIRED. Backlist: Tammy Duckworth, Condoleezza Rice, Dana Perino, Maureen Dowd. Mission: "singular books by authors with unique perspectives and compelling authority." This book fits the mission statement precisely. Lead pitch: the analytical rigor differentiates it from the memoir wave. Approach through agent only.

outreach.name: Keith Urbahn — Javelin
outreach.role: Literary agent — national security, political nonfiction, D.C. insider books

notes.text: Javelin DC, Old Town Alexandria VA. urbahn@javelindc.com (verify current). Co-founder with Matt Latimer. Former Rumsfeld chief of staff, Yale, U.S. Navy reserve intelligence officer. Clients: James Comey, Tucker Carlson, Don Rumsfeld. BEST FIT: national security + D.C. insider nonfiction is his exact lane. He has cleared national security review himself. Approach: lead with the structural/analytical differentiation from the Elizondo memoir wave. Mention June 9 Capitol event and the Turner/NAA document as the hook. Query first — 1-2 page letter + brief sample.

outreach.name: Eric Lupfer — Calligraph
outreach.role: Literary agent — national security, defense, intelligence, history

notes.text: Calligraph Literary Agency, New York & Boston. Submissions: submissions@calligraphlit.com — include agent name in subject line, query + 25 pages in body (no attachments). Formerly WME (14 years), Fletcher & Co, UTA. Moved to Calligraph 2025. Explicitly lists "national security and defense" and "military, intelligence, and national defense professionals" as focus areas. Army brat background. Fastest query response on record (1 day turnaround per QueryTracker). STRONG FIT. Approach: query emphasizes primary-source rigor, classification architecture argument, and the June 9 Capitol event.

outreach.name: Matt Latimer — Javelin
outreach.role: Literary agent — political nonfiction, White House/Pentagon books

notes.text: Javelin DC — co-founder with Keith Urbahn. Former Bush White House speechwriter, Rumsfeld chief speechwriter, Columbia journalism MA, Michigan Law JD. Clients: political figures, historians, journalists. Could query Urbahn or Latimer at Javelin — both have national security background. Query both simultaneously or choose one. Approach same as Urbahn query.
